# gem4d

> A Gemma 4 12B inference engine in Go + CUDA C++, built for exactly one target: the NVIDIA DGX
> Spark **GB10** (Blackwell, `sm_121a`, CUDA 13.0). Loads Q4_0 (QAT) / Q8_0 GGUF weights, runs the
> full forward pass on-GPU with FP8 Tensor-Core attention, and serves an OpenAI-compatible HTTP
> API plus one-shot and interactive CLI modes. Hardware-specific and experimental.

The model is Gemma 4 12B: 48 layers (40 sliding-window + 8 global attention), GeGLU FFN
(`gelu_pytorch_tanh`, intermediate 15360), RoPE/p-RoPE, logit softcap 30.0, vocab 262144, a Q6_K
tied LM head, hidden 3840. Decode on GB10 is **memory-bandwidth-bound on total weight bytes**: the
levers that move it are CUDA-graph launch-bubble removal and speculation acceptance (τ), not
weight-load width.

## Architecture (bottom-up)

- [cuda/gemma4_kernels.cu](cuda/gemma4_kernels.cu): the entire dense-12B forward pass — quantized
  GEMV/GEMM (Q4_0/Q6_K/Q8_0 dp4a, warp-per-row), FP8 flash attention, RoPE, RMSNorm, GeGLU, GPU
  sampling, and the CUDA-graph capture/replay. Compiled to `cuda/libgem4d.a`.
- [cuda/gemma4_kernels.cuh](cuda/gemma4_kernels.cuh): model dimensions and the engine struct.
- [internal/engine/cuda/bridge.go](internal/engine/cuda/bridge.go): the CGO bridge — wraps the
  opaque CUDA engine as a Go `Engine` (`NewEngine`, `Prefill`, `Decode`, `DecodeNoCopy`,
  `SampleDevice`, `GenerateSpec`, `LoadAssistant`, `SpecStats`, …).
- [internal/server](internal/server): OpenAI-compatible HTTP server, gemma-4 chat template, tool-call
  parsing, thinking channel, prefix-reuse KV cache.
- [internal/tokenizer](internal/tokenizer): Gemma 4 SentencePiece/Unigram tokenizer.
- [cmd/gem4d](cmd/gem4d): CLI entry point and the three run modes (`main.go`, `oneshot.go`, `args.go`).

## Build & run

- [Makefile](Makefile): `make` builds `cuda/libgem4d.a` (nvcc `-arch=sm_121a`) then the Go binary;
  it verifies the cubin arch and the device-upload code path (cgo does not hash the static archive).
- [README.md](README.md): full build, usage, flags, HTTP API, and performance summary.
- Run: `gem4d -m model.gguf --ctx 32768 --port 8080` (server) or `gem4d -m model.gguf -p "Hi" -n 100`.
- Smoke test: `make smoke`. CPU tests: `go test ./internal/server/ ./internal/tokenizer/`.

## Decode performance facts

- **CUDA graphs** are position-independent (device-resident position, KV writes inside the graph)
  and cover single-token decode + the K-row batched speculative-verify forward; both replayed every
  step. Pre-captured at startup by `Warmup()`. Opt out: `GEM4D_NO_DECODE_GRAPH=1`,
  `GEM4D_NO_BATCHED_GRAPH=1`.
- **Packed-Q4_0** (`[16-B quants ‖ fp16 scales]`, coalesced `uint4` loads) is default-on, bit-exact,
  opt out `GEM4D_NO_PACKED=1`. Net ~neutral on single-token decode — decode is bandwidth-bound, so
  wider loads cut instruction count but not bytes. Do not expect a speedup from load width here.
- **FP8 E4M3 KV cache** (1 B/elem). Attention is not the gap; weight-load bandwidth is.
- Observe speculation acceptance live in `/metrics` → `speculation` block (`accept_rate`,
  `tokens_per_forward`). τ is the highest-leverage decode tunable.

## Benchmarks

- [scripts/pi_bench.py](scripts/pi_bench.py): fair side-by-side vs an OpenAI-compatible server
  (divergence-free transcript) — the canonical decode/prefill head-to-head.
- [scripts/parity_bench.py](scripts/parity_bench.py): bit-exact fingerprint + suffix-prefill parity.
- [scripts/benchmark_gem4.py](scripts/benchmark_gem4.py): prefill-decay, decode-rate, tool-loop.

## Optional / separate efforts

- DiffusionGemma (block text-diffusion MoE) is a **separate, in-progress** engine (`cuda/diffusion_*`,
  `internal/engine/diffusion/`, `cmd/gem4d/diffusion.go`) — not part of the dense-12B path above.
- NVFP4 / FP4 tensor-core GEMM (`GEM4D_FP4`) is experimental prefill work, separate from dense decode.
